frameworks.name: Bayesian decision theory
frameworks.one_line: Mathematical framework for ideal performance benchmarks in visuomotor tasks.

frameworks.name: process model of visuomotor processing
frameworks.one_line: Question whether BDT also describes the mechanism, not just the benchmark.

frameworks.name: cue combination
frameworks.one_line: Near-optimal cue combination achievable via process unlike BDT.

core_question: What experimental criteria meaningfully test whether visuomotor perception is itself Bayesian inference, rather than merely approximating its benchmark?
key_assumptions: Ideal performance in visuomotor tasks is computable from BDT.
A model can match near-optimal performance without resembling BDT.
Claims of BDT as process model require stronger criteria than fit to ideal.
manipulations: Conceptual comparison of human performance to BDT-ideal benchmark.
Proposed transfer between novel tasks in familiar environments and familiar tasks in novel environments.
key_findings: Near-optimal cue-combination performance can arise from a process model bearing little resemblance to BDT.
Comparison to ideal performance, while necessary, is a weak experimental test of BDT-as-process.
Recent motor-control work can be reinterpreted as tests of BDT transfer properties.
interpretations: Transfer criteria operationalize what it means to claim perception is Bayesian inference.
Transfer is especially relevant for natural scenes and rapid adaptation across novel task-environment pairings.
limitations_noted: Transfer criteria are described as only the beginning of an operationalization.
